**Plan Document Present And Linked**

Pass. The planning issue requires `dev/active/2e926e39-906e-4952-8ae0-38215a7e5aac-plan.md`, and `issue.documents` links that exact path. The file exists and is non-empty; its heading and note identify planning node `8efc0d75` and criteria source `2e926e39` at [dev/active/2e926e39-906e-4952-8ae0-38215a7e5aac-plan.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/2e926e39-906e-4952-8ae0-38215a7e5aac-plan.md:1).

**Completeness Against Criteria**

Fail. The plan enumerates REQ-01 through REQ-19 and maps each to an item at [plan.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/2e926e39-906e-4952-8ae0-38215a7e5aac-plan.md:181), so there is no obvious omitted criterion.

Blocking issue: REQ-13 is addressed only at the goal level, not as an executable design. The plan says to “extend the `plan` template … so applying `plan` also requires `repo-validate` on the `container` anchor,” with fallback “via the apply-plan / epic-creation flow” if anchor gates are unsupported ([plan.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/2e926e39-906e-4952-8ae0-38215a7e5aac-plan.md:23), [plan.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/2e926e39-906e-4952-8ae0-38215a7e5aac-plan.md:143), [plan.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/2e926e39-906e-4952-8ae0-38215a7e5aac-plan.md:212)). Current template support has no anchor-gate concept: `GraphTemplate` contains `anchors`, `nodes`, `anchor_edges`, and `transforms`, while `gates` exist only on `TemplateNode` ([crates/jit/src/templates.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/templates.rs:205), [crates/jit/src/templates.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/templates.rs:273)). The shipped `plan` template likewise declares gates only on created planning/breakdown nodes, not on the container anchor ([.jit/templates.toml](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.jit/templates.toml:17)). Because the fallback does not specify which command/flow is changed, when it attaches the gate, or how it avoids hardcoding container semantics, the hard requirement “required on a breakable container … and blocks transition to done” is under-designed.

**Technical Soundness And Architectural Fit**

Mostly sound, with the REQ-13 blocker above. The plan correctly reuses existing seams for most changes: `issue create` currently has required `--title` and no `--type` ([crates/jit/src/cli.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/cli.rs:433)); `gate check`/`pass` are positional-only today ([crates/jit/src/cli.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/cli.rs:972)); `query` exposes `all` and `available` but no bare default or `ready` alias ([crates/jit/src/cli.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/cli.rs:1455)); `graph rdeps` defaults to unbounded depth via `default_value = "0"` ([crates/jit/src/cli.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/cli.rs:1356)); `run_rules(Some(id))` exists for single-issue validation ([crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs:908)); and the apply engine auto-binds the container anchor but currently just delegates to `apply_template` ([crates/jit/src/main.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/main.rs:1869)).

The architectural problem is that REQ-13’s proposed “template anchor gate” is not merely absent; it crosses a model boundary the current schema does not have. The plan needs a concrete schema extension such as anchor-level gates, or a concrete apply-time attachment rule that is explicitly generic and testable.

**Decomposition And Dependencies**

Mostly coherent. The GateCommands-heavy work is serialized through the chain called out in the risk table: grammar standard → first-guess gate fixes → `--gate` flag → check history → update → rename sweep ([plan.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/2e926e39-906e-4952-8ae0-38215a7e5aac-plan.md:210)). That avoids obvious textual and semantic conflicts around the same enum.

Blocking gap: the “Repo-wide integrity gate at container completion” item depends only on “Per-issue validation scoping” ([plan.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/2e926e39-906e-4952-8ae0-38215a7e5aac-plan.md:143)), but it may require template-schema or apply-engine changes. Those prerequisites are not represented as dependencies or sub-work. An implementer could satisfy the item’s written blast radius by editing `.jit/templates.toml`, only to find the loader cannot express anchor gates and the apply engine only attaches gates to created nodes via `attach_node_gates` ([crates/jit/src/commands/template.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/template.rs:592)).

**Risks And Actionability**

Fail. The risk table identifies the REQ-13 uncertainty, but its mitigation is not actionable enough: “the breakdown item chooses and cites which path the template engine permits” ([plan.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/2e926e39-906e-4952-8ae0-38215a7e5aac-plan.md:212)). This leaves a load-bearing design decision to the breakdown/implementation phase. For a planning issue, the plan should decide the path now: either add anchor-gates to the template schema and loader, or attach `repo-validate` in `jit apply plan`/container creation through a specified generic mechanism, with exact tests.

Other risks are adequately handled: the CLI rename blast radius has a grep acceptance check ([plan.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/2e926e39-906e-4952-8ae0-38215a7e5aac-plan.md:203)), and REQ-12 correctly distinguishes `jit validate <id>` from subtree `--scope` ([plan.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/dev/active/2e926e39-906e-4952-8ae0-38215a7e5aac-plan.md:211)).

**Prior Review Feedback**

`run_history` is empty, so there is no prior feedback to verify.

VERDICT: FAIL
